'''Blorga''' was a [[Gladiator/Legends|gladiator]] who participated in free-for-all mortal brawls in the [[The Cauldron|Cauldron]] arena, the largest combat pit on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Rattatak/Legends|Rattatak]]. During the final years of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Old Republic]], Blorga fought in the Cauldron when [[Asajj Ventress/Legends|Asajj Ventress]] [[Mission to Rattatak|entered]] the fray and ultimately emerged victorious over the other gladiators.

==Biography==
Shortly after the outbreak of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> Blorga fought in [[the Cauldron]] on [[Rattatak/Legends|Rattatak]].<ref name="CW" /> It was the largest gladiatorial pit on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]], filled with violent warriors, where war barons and generals sought out the best combatants who would win their wars.<ref name="DBR">{{DB|location|rattatak|Rattatak}}</ref> Therefore, Blorga, like many other gladiators,<ref name="CW">{{CW|6}}</ref> entered a deadly free-for-all battle,<ref name="EGD">''[[The New Essential Guide to Droids]]''</ref> but it was a rare gladiator indeed who could survive multiple fights there.<ref name="DBR" />

[[File:Blorga cauldron.jpg|300px|right|thumb|Blorga destroys a Bugnaught in The Cauldron]]
During one battle, the Gamorrean fought the [[Aqualish/Legends|Aqualish]] [[Grunda Dolma]] and later destroyed a [[Bugnaught]] combat [[Droid/Legends|droid]]. When the [[Dathomirian/Legends|Dathomirian]] [[Asajj Ventress/Legends|Asajj Ventress]] entered the arena, Dolma and Blorga prepared to attack the newcomer; however, the Dathomirian used [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to [[Telekinesis/Legends|throw]] the pair back. Shortly afterward, the Gamorrean warrior stood alongside a group of gladiators, all of whom were defeated by Ventress.<ref name="CW" />

==Personality and traits==
Blorga was a [[Gamorrean/Legends|Gamorrean]] with green skin and [[Color/Legends|red]] [[Eye/Legends|eyes]]. Gamorreans were dull-witted fighters, but what they lacked in speed they more than made up for in savagery.<ref name="Sticker" /> 

==Equipment==
While in the Cauldron, Blorga wore two [[Pauldron/Legends|pauldrons]], a metal [[Helmet/Legends|helmet]], a loincloth, and a chestplate. The gladiator fought with a double-spiked hammer.<ref name="CW" />

==Behind the scenes==
Blorga<ref name="Sticker" /> first appeared as an unnamed background character in the [[Chapter 6 (Star Wars: Clone Wars)|sixth chapter]]<ref name="CW" /> of ''[[Star Wars: Clone Wars]]'', an animated television micro-series<ref name="Chapter Archive">{{SWArchive|url=clonewars/microseries/chapter/archive.html|text=View ''Clone Wars'' Chapter Archive}}</ref> produced by [[Cartoon Network]] Studios<ref name="Volume One">[[Star Wars: Clone Wars Volume One|''Star Wars: Clone Wars'' Volume One]]</ref> and originally aired on [[November 14]], [[2003]].<ref name="TV">{{TVcom|url=shows/star-wars-clone-wars/chapter-vi-276612|text=Star Wars: Clone Wars - Season 1, Episode 6: Chapter VI}}</ref> The character was later given a name in ''[[Ultimate Sticker Book: Star Wars: Clone Wars]]'', a sticker book written by [[Simon Beecroft]] and released in [[2004]].<ref name="DK">{{DK|url=us/9780756607593-ultimate-sticker-book-star-wars-clone-wars|text=Ultimate Sticker Book: Star Wars: Clone Wars|nolive=1}}</ref>
